description.value:
Source: Ariadne's Book of Legends - Alpha v1.2, p. 130.

Theophrastus’ Flux

10th-level transmutation

Classes: Bard, Sorcerer, Warlock, Wizard Casting Time: 1 action Range: Self (15-foot cone) Components: V, S, M (a transmutation stone worth at least 5,000 GP) Duration: Concentration, up to 3 rounds

You manifest a formidable pulse of volatile transmutation in your hand, nourishing it before setting it loose in a 15-foot cone in front of you. The pulse transforms everything in its path into a nonmagical material of your choice, such as gold, glass, wood, or sand. Creatures in the area must make a Constitution saving throw against the spell, taking 8d6 x 10 force damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one. A creature entirely contained within the area of the spell has disadvantage on the saving throw. If this spell reduces a creature to 0 hit points, it is completely transformed into the chosen material. Magic items that are of Legendary, Artifact, or Relic rarity are not transformed by this spell.

When you cast this spell, you can hold it to increase its range and potency, releasing it in one of your subsequent turns by using an action. To maintain such a powerful arcanism, you must expend your action on each of your turns to hold the spell. If you hold it for 1 round, the range becomes a 30-foot cone instead, and the damage increases to 8d8 x 10. If you hold it for 2 rounds, you can choose to make the range a 60-foot cone, and the damage increases to 8d12 x 10. If you

hold it for 3 rounds, you can choose to make the range a 90-foot cone, and the damage increases to 8d20 x 10.

If you lose concentration on this spell before you release it, you regain the Legendary Points you spent on its channeling, but you lose the 10th-level spell slot.

source.custom: Original en-US text from Alpha v1.2

materials.value: A transmutation stone worth at least 5,000 gp
